canonical_definition: Based on the provided literature, essay scoring is characterized as a task of automatic evaluation. It is defined as the process of automatically assessing the writing skills of a language learner, specifically as those skills are demonstrated in a text they have produced. This task is also referred to as automated essay scoring (AES). The central goal, as described in the source material, is to evaluate a learner's writing abilities based on their written output.
papers: CBP-Tuning: Efficient Local Customization for Black-box Large Language Models
Hallucination Detection inLLMs Using Spectral Features of Attention Maps
Language models can learn implicit multi-hop reasoning, but only if they have lots of training data

definitions.text: The task of automatically evaluating the writing skills of a language learner as reflected in a text they have produced.
definitions.paper: Language models can learn implicit multi-hop reasoning, but only if they have lots of training data

This task is also referred to as automated essay scoring (AES). The central goal, as described in the source material, is to evaluate a learner's writing abilities based on their written output.
